selection_rule.candidate_source: all protocol-v2 and protocol-v3 threshold circuits
selection_rule.candidate_exposure: candidate circuits were extracted only on their protocol development data and never on the v4 gate
selection_rule.development_domain: exact union of protocol-v3 development and gate prompts; all 768 are permanently burned as gate material
selection_rule.choice: among circuits exact against P(x) on all 768 development prompts, maximize the minimum per-example signed correct-token margin; break ties by minimum edge count, then lower threshold
selection_rule.justification: bracket_type circuits were exact on development but missed 9/256 and then 1/256 on consecutive fresh gates, while the full model and quote circuit remained exact; robustness-first selection targets boundary slack rather than parsimony
selection_rule.fallback: if no existing threshold circuit is exact, rerun extraction only for the failing task on the 768-prompt development domain
selection_rule.gate_use: the final 512-prompt gate is evaluated only after selection and never changes the selected circuit or program subset

stop_policy.on_v4_gate_failure: stop the exact-generalization track; report measured per-task and per-stratum gate agreement as a near-exact faithfulness result, scope all claims accordingly, and do not heal or verify an exact circuit claim
stop_policy.larger_circuit_policy: larger or lower-threshold circuits require a separately preregistered future study with new evaluation data; they are not selected against the v4 gate

retrospective_diagnostic.purpose: record the failing prompt stratum and every candidate circuit margin, including whether an alternative exact-on-v3-development candidate would have classified it correctly

provenance.protocol_v2_result: bracket_type circuit scored 247/256 on the untouched v2 gate
provenance.protocol_v3_result: bracket_type circuit scored 255/256 on the untouched v3 gate; full model and quote circuit remained exact
provenance.burned_rows: all 768 protocol-v3 development plus gate prompts are v4 development data and are permanently unavailable as gate material
provenance.protocol_v4_gate_source: the next 512 deterministic generator rows after all 768 burned prompts, balanced 256/256 across each task's two classes

notes: No model predictions are used to generate, select, or filter examples.
The v4 gate is the final untouched gate and is not used to select circuits, programs, or program subsets.
Protocol-v2 and protocol-v3 artifacts remain unchanged as failed held-out-generalization records.
